questions.q: How does the author portray the idea of childhood in the poem 'The Child is Father of the Man'?
questions.answer: The author portrays the idea of childhood as a time of innocence, imagination, and creativity.

questions.q:
Read the extract: 'The child is father of the man...'
What does the phrase 'father of the man' signify?
questions.answer: The phrase 'father of the man' signifies that the child is the source of adult personality and values.

questions.q: What is the significance of the phrase 'The Child is Father of the Man'?
questions.answer: The child is the foundation of adult personality.

questions.q:
Read the extract: 'I had a little nut tree, nobody own it, nobody tend it...'
What does the tree symbolize?
questions.answer: The tree symbolizes the freedom and independence of childhood.

questions.q: How does the poem 'The Little Nut Tree' portray the idea of childhood?
questions.answer: The poem portrays the idea of childhood as a time of freedom, imagination, and creativity.

questions.q: What is the significance of the image of a child playing in the poem 'The Child is Father of the Man'?
questions.answer: The image of a child playing signifies the carefree and innocent nature of childhood.

questions.q:
Read the extract: 'My mother used to tell me...'
What does the extract suggest about the narrator's childhood?
questions.answer: The extract suggests that the narrator's childhood was marked by a close relationship with his mother.

questions.q: What is the central theme of the poem 'The Little Nut Tree'?
questions.answer: The central theme of the poem is the freedom and independence of childhood.

questions.q: How does the author use imagery in the poem 'The Child is Father of the Man' to portray the idea of childhood?
questions.answer: The author uses imagery to portray the idea of childhood as a time of innocence, imagination, and creativity.

questions.q: What is the significance of the phrase 'nobody own it, nobody tend it' in the poem 'The Little Nut Tree'?
questions.answer: The phrase signifies the freedom and independence of childhood.
